description: PR-10.3 quiz presentation-randomization review (companion to pr10-3-presentation-randomization.md).
method: operator adversarial self-audit of cc_spine/presentation.py + the quiz render CLI path plus a test suite, THEN an INDEPENDENT agent code review dispatched and COMPLETED before commit per governance §16.0. The reviewer read presentation.py, quiz.py, cli.py, tests, Makefile, and the workflow, and empirically exercised edge cases, end-to-end grading, and type handling.

independent_findings.problem: the range guard accepted Python bool (bool is an int subclass), so grade_presented(..., True) graded the submission as displayed index 1 (False as 0) instead of rejecting a malformed submission.
independent_findings.resolution: added _is_index (isinstance int and not isinstance bool); grade_presented now rejects bool/float/str/None fail-closed. Test test_bool_and_non_int_display_are_rejected added.

independent_findings.problem: anti-memorization is contingent on the caller supplying a fresh per-attempt seed, but the CLI default was a FIXED seed 'attempt-0' and the module docstring claimed 'the letters move every attempt' unconditionally — with a reused seed the order is identical and fully memorizable.
independent_findings.resolution: the CLI default was removed; 'quiz render' now REQUIRES --attempt-seed and exits 2 with guidance if absent. The module docstring and report were re-scoped to the load-bearing seed-freshness contract. Test test_cli_render_requires_a_seed added.

independent_findings.problem: the anti-memorization gate asserted only '>1 distinct slot over 30 seeds for one item', which permits heavy single-slot bias and exercised a single item; weak proof of 'not consistently pinned to one letter'.
independent_findings.resolution: strengthened: over 200 seeds across 6 multi-choice items, the correct answer must reach EVERY display slot and no slot may exceed 60% share. (Reviewer measured the SHA-256 rank as uniform in expectation; the stronger gate now guards against a future regression.)

independent_findings.problem: the mapping primitive indexed the order with no range check and no documented precondition; a direct out-of-range/negative call raises IndexError or wraps.
independent_findings.resolution: documented the [0,n) precondition explicitly and noted grade_presented is the guarded public entry point.

independent_findings.problem: presentation_id was described as correlating a render with its grading, but nothing verified it — a seed mix-up between render and grade would silently map through the wrong permutation.
independent_findings.resolution: grade_presented now accepts an optional expected_presentation_id and fails closed on a mismatch; presentation_id docstring reframed to the integrity-check role it now actually serves. Test test_presentation_id_mismatch_is_rejected added.

clean_areas_confirmed_by_reviewer: canonical immutability: learner_view returns a fresh dict, render_item REBINDS choices to a new list (no in-place mutation), choice elements are immutable strings — goldset/holdout untouched (verified by before/after JSON compare)
presentation_order is a genuine permutation for all n incl n=0 ([]) and n=1 ([0]); render_item does not break on 0/1-choice items

mapping direction consistent end-to-end: order[displayed]==canonical for render and for grading; correct choice grades correct, every other displayed slot grades wrong (verified empirically across many seeds)
determinism: sha256 hexdigest ranks + stable sort; no builtin hash(), no set ordering, no random, no wall clock

leak surface: render is strictly learner_view allowlist + presentation_id + choice_count; no correct_index/rationale/fact_ids/expected_keywords/answer_key_ref/permutation; presentation_id does not encode order; a learner with item_id+seed can recompute the ORDER but not which canonical index is correct, so the learner-visible seed is not an answer leak
item_hash / answer_key_hash pin canonical content and are untouched by rendering
holdout uses identical machinery with no answer/order field and grades correctly
non-int (float) and out-of-range indices rejected (bool edge was F1, now fixed)
note: Independent review completed BEFORE this commit (the PR-9 lesson). Verdict accept-with-fixes; all five findings (2 minor input/contract, 1 minor test-strength, 2 nit) applied before commit. The reviewer flagged three overstated docstring/report claims (per-attempt movement, presentation_id correlation, gate-7 strength) — all corrected in prose and, where applicable, in code.
